content.problemStatement:
Given nnn (nnn is **even**), determine the number of **black cells** in an n×nn \times nn×n chessboard.


Input Format
The only line of the input contains a single integer nnn.



Output Format
Output the number of black cells in an n×nn \times nn×n chessboard.
content.inputFormat: A single line containing one even integer n (2 ≤ n ≤ 100).
content.outputFormat: A single integer – the number of black cells on an n×n chessboard.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 8×8 = 64 cells, half of them are black, so 64/2 = 32.

solution.approach: The chessboard has n·n cells. Exactly half of them are black because the colors alternate and n is even, so the answer is (n·n)/2. Compute the product using 64‑bit integer to avoid overflow and output the integer division result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) – only a few arithmetic operations.

code.Python3:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
out = []
for token in data:
    n = int(token)
    out.append(str((n * n) // 2))
print('\n'.join(out))
